speaker: YORK
text: Are not the speedy scouts returned again

speaker: YORK
text: That dogged the mighty army of the Dauphin?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They are returned, my lord, and give it out

speaker: MESSENGER
text: That he is marched to Bordeaux with his power

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To fight with Talbot. As he marched along,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: By your espials were discoverèd

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Two mightier troops than that the Dauphin led,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Which joined with him and made their march for Bordeaux.

speaker: YORK
text: A plague upon that villain Somerset

speaker: YORK
text: That thus delays my promisèd supply

speaker: YORK
text: Of horsemen that were levied for this siege!

speaker: YORK
text: Renownèd Talbot doth expect my aid,

speaker: YORK
text: And I am louted by a traitor villain

speaker: YORK
text: And cannot help the noble chevalier.

speaker: YORK
text: God comfort him in this necessity.

speaker: YORK
text: If he miscarry, farewell wars in France.

speaker: LUCY
text: Thou princely leader of our English strength,

speaker: LUCY
text: Never so needful on the earth of France,

speaker: LUCY
text: Spur to the rescue of the noble Talbot,

speaker: LUCY
text: Who now is girdled with a waist of iron

speaker: LUCY
text: And hemmed about with grim destruction.

speaker: LUCY
text: To Bordeaux, warlike duke! To Bordeaux, York!

speaker: LUCY
text: Else farewell Talbot, France, and England’s honor.

speaker: YORK
text: O God, that Somerset, who in proud heart

speaker: YORK
text: Doth stop my cornets, were in Talbot’s place!

speaker: YORK
text: So should we save a valiant gentleman

speaker: YORK
text: By forfeiting a traitor and a coward.

speaker: YORK
text: Mad ire and wrathful fury makes me weep

speaker: YORK
text: That thus we die while remiss traitors sleep.

speaker: LUCY
text: O, send some succor to the distressed lord!

speaker: YORK
text: He dies, we lose; I break my warlike word;

speaker: YORK
text: We mourn, France smiles; we lose, they daily get,

speaker: YORK
text: All long of this vile traitor Somerset.

speaker: LUCY
text: Then God take mercy on brave Talbot’s soul,

speaker: LUCY
text: And on his son, young John, who two hours since

speaker: LUCY
text: I met in travel toward his warlike father.

speaker: LUCY
text: This seven years did not Talbot see his son,

speaker: LUCY
text: And now they meet where both their lives are done.

speaker: YORK
text: Alas, what joy shall noble Talbot have

speaker: YORK
text: To bid his young son welcome to his grave?

speaker: YORK
text: Away! Vexation almost stops my breath,

speaker: YORK
text: That sundered friends greet in the hour of death.

speaker: YORK
text: Lucy, farewell. No more my fortune can

speaker: YORK
text: But curse the cause I cannot aid the man.

speaker: YORK
text: Maine, Blois, Poictiers, and Tours are won away,

speaker: YORK
text: Long all of Somerset and his delay.

speaker: LUCY
text: Thus while the vulture of sedition

speaker: LUCY
text: Feeds in the bosom of such great commanders,

speaker: LUCY
text: Sleeping neglection doth betray to loss

speaker: LUCY
text: The conquest of our scarce-cold conqueror,

speaker: LUCY
text: That ever-living man of memory,

speaker: LUCY
text: Henry the Fifth. Whiles they each other cross,

speaker: LUCY
text: Lives, honors, lands, and all hurry to loss.
